title: Simple Side Table or Nightstand
summary: A two-shelf 2x4-leg table built in an afternoon from three boards — works as a bedside table against a wall or a floating side table with a trimmed back edge.
description:
This is the table you build when you need a nightstand THIS weekend: two leg sets, a 1x10 lower shelf, a 1x12 top shelf, done. No joinery beyond screws or brads, and no board wider than what a home center sells off the shelf.

The only real technique in the whole build is keeping the two leg sets square to each other as you attach the shelves — if the shelf boards' edges don't line up flush on both sides as you screw them down, that's your signal the legs went in slightly twisted, and it's worth backing a screw out and correcting it now rather than living with a table that rocks.

The top shelf has two build options depending on where the table lives. Against a wall as a true bedside table, cut both 1x12 top boards to the same 22" length, start with the front board flush to the front and both sides, then let the back board overhang the back edge by about 1/2" — nobody sees that edge. As a floating side table or the end of a sofa run where every side is visible, trim that back overhang flush with a circular saw instead.

Choose your fastening method by finish: star-bit screws are easy to fill and hide under paint and pull tighter, while brad nails and a generous bead of glue disappear better under a stain finish where you don't want visible filled holes.

materials.name: 2x4, 8 ft or stud length
materials.note: All four legs and side supports.

materials.name: Self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2" to 2", or brad nails and glue
materials.note: Screws are easier to fill under paint; brads and glue hide better under stain.

cutList.note: Against a wall: back board overhangs 1/2". Fully visible placement: trim the back edge flush instead.

steps.title: Build the two leg sets
steps.body: Attach a leg to a side support, then repeat for the other three corners, building two matching leg-and-support pairs. Two 2-1/2" screws per joint.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body:
Attach the two 1x10 bottom shelf boards inside the leg sets so their outside edges sit flush with the legs. Use either 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, or the shorter star bit screws — screws are easier to fill and hide for a paint finish, nails are easier to hide for a stain finish (use a generous amount of glue either way).

Check that the legs are square to each other as you go: if the 1x10 edges don't line up flush on both sides, the legs went in slightly twisted, and it's worth correcting now.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2" to 2", or brad nails and glue

steps.title: Attach the top shelf
steps.body:
Repeat the same fastening method for the top 1x12 boards. For a true bedside table where the back sits against a wall, start with the front board flush on the front and both sides, then let the back board overhang the back edge by about 1/2" — that edge is never seen. For a floating side table or an end table where every side shows, trim that back overhang flush with a circular saw instead.

Sand and finish to match the room.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 1-1/2" to 2", or brad nails and glue

images.alt: Simple two-shelf side table on 2x4 legs
